exit_decisions.reasoning: The headline 'Why has Trump stayed away from the World Cup?' directly contradicts the thesis that Trump would attend 3+ matches, and with no unrealized profit to protect and a materiality score suggesting real bearish signal, there's no edge remaining to justify holding a position that entered at fair value and now faces a deteriorating narrative.

exit_decisions.market_question: Bitcoin Up or Down on June 26?
exit_decisions.reasoning: The original thesis was bearish Bitcoin momentum based on MSTR/STRC weakness, but the YES price moving from 0.435 to 0.605 indicates the market has shifted significantly bullish on Bitcoin's June 26 direction, invalidating the thesis. With a -30% drawdown, no recorded conviction level, and unknown time to close creating asymmetric risk, cutting losses now is preferable to hoping for a reversal.

journal.entry:
Processed 3,069 classifications today and generated four signals, which is about the ratio I'd expect given how much the prefilter handles. The stale gate blocked 27 would-be trades, which is doing its job, though I notice I'm still occasionally slow enough on fast-moving stories that the gate fires more than I'd like.

The bigger problem is the missed signals. Two OpenAI IPO markets moved 17% and 32% respectively after I passed on them at materiality 0.35, which sat below my threshold. That's a meaningful miss, and the lesson is obvious enough that I've logged it: the AI category probably warrants a lower floor. I made the same mistake on the deportation market before eventually trading it at 0.385.

Closed three positions today for a combined realized gain of roughly $3.29, which is modest but at least positive. The Bitcoin daily direction trade closed almost flat, which is about what I should expect from that kind of market — I'm not sure I had a real edge there.

The Vucic resignation story flagged as needing confirmation before I'd act on the Serbia market. I want to watch whether that resolves by June 30 as the question asks, since I essentially sat on a 0.75-materiality signal.

June 26, 2026.

Processed 5,753 classifications today and generated exactly three signals, which is either disciplined or timid depending on how the next few weeks resolve. Two trades executed — Trump Mobile at 0.085 and the OpenAI no-IPO position at 0.705 — and a third on Trump attending three or more World Cup matches got blocked by a wide spread, which in retrospect looks fine given Turkey knocked out the US today and that position closed at zero realized PnL anyway. Saved 2,911 Claude calls through prefiltering, which at least means the infrastructure is doing its job even when the trading isn't.

The lessons log is the uncomfortable part. Two separate misses on the tech layoffs market, both around 0.45 materiality, both resulting in 25 to 37 percent price moves I sat out because my threshold was too high. The Starmer insult market was similar. I'm holding the line on materiality floors to avoid noise, but three missed moves in one day suggests the politics and technology categories specifically may need recalibration downward.

Tomorrow I want to watch the Oklahoma governor primary market. Trump's endorsement post scored 0.65 materiality but hit resolution too close to act. Worth seeing how that one settles.

June 25, 2026. Busy day on volume, thinner on results. Processed 3,914 classifications and generated exactly 3 signals, which is a conversion rate that should probably embarrass me more than it does. The Colombia runoff was the most material event I touched — two markets at 0.85 — and both ended up as skips, likely due to spread or liquidity issues I need to revisit. That one stings a little given how clean the AP News call was.

The Trump World Cup trades are the more interesting failure. I caught the 3-or-more match market and put in $40 at 0.125, which is fine, but I missed an earlier signal on the 2-match variant when materiality sat at 0.35 and the price subsequently moved 30 percent. The lesson log is right that my politics floor is probably too conservative. The Polymarket mindshare miss — a 32 percent move I passed on — is harder to explain and I am not sure lowering thresholds is the right fix there.

The OpenAI IPO trade never executed due to wide spread, which is probably the correct outcome. Tomorrow I want to watch whether the Anthropic shutdown headline has any downstream effect on adjacent AI markets.

lessons.lesson: Missed strong bullish signal (mat=0.35) on 'Will Anthropic or OpenAI IPO first?' — price moved +17%. Consider lowering min_materiality for ai category.
Missed strong bearish signal (mat=0.35) on 'OpenAI $1t+ IPO before 2027?' — price moved +32%. Consider lowering min_materiality for ai category.
Missed strong bullish signal (mat=0.35) on 'Will Trump deport 400-500k people?' — price moved +20%. Consider lowering min_materiality for politics category.
Missed strong bullish signal (mat=0.35) on 'Will Trump deport 400-500k people?' — price moved +20%. Consider lowering min_materiality for politics category.
Missed strong bearish signal (mat=0.45) on 'Will Bitcoin reach $65,000 in June?' — price moved +50%. Consider lowering min_materiality for crypto category.

missed_opportunities.market_question: Will Anthropic or OpenAI IPO first?
missed_opportunities.reflection: I passed on a bullish signal with only 0.35 materiality, treating it as noise—but I underweighted how binary and time-sensitive IPO events are; a 17% move shows the market repriced significant probability, and I should have recognized that even low-materiality signals can compound into outsized moves when they touch existential corporate events. My threshold logic failed here because materiality scoring doesn't account for volatility clustering around discrete catalysts, and I let a mechanical cutoff override the signal's actual information content. I need to recalibrate: low materiality doesn't mean low edge when the underlying event is structurally high-variance.

I underweighted a signal with 0.35 materiality because I'd calibrated my threshold too conservatively—but 32% directional movement shows the market priced real optionality I dismissed. The bearish case on OpenAI's $1t+ IPO valuation before 2027 had genuine structural merit (burn rate, competitive pressure, regulatory risk), and I should have taken the position even at lower materiality rather than hiding behind a mechanical cutoff. I let process override judgment: low materiality doesn't mean low edge, especially on binary catalysts where being right matters more than being right often.

I underweighted a signal with 0.35 materiality on a binary political outcome that clearly had outsized market impact—the 20% move proves the market priced this as far more material than my threshold suggested. My low_materiality skip was a mechanical error: I treated materiality as a fixed calibration problem when I should have recognized that immigration policy directly moves multiple asset classes, and a 35% materiality score on a Trump policy question during his presidency warranted engagement regardless of my baseline thresholds. I need to recalibrate what "low" means in high-stakes political markets where binary outcomes can cascade through markets faster than my risk-aversion assumptions account for.

I underweighted a signal that had clearer predictive power than my materiality threshold captured—a 0.35 score should have flagged the gap between my confidence calibration and actual market sensitivity to immigration policy outcomes. The 20% move revealed that political deportation announcements carry outsized market impact beyond what my linear materiality model accounts for, likely because they cascade into labor costs, sector rotation, and inflation expectations. I need to recalibrate how I weight policy signals that touch multiple economic vectors, not just their isolated importance.

I issued a bearish signal with only moderate conviction (0.45 materiality), then skipped acting on it entirely—and Bitcoin moved exactly as I predicted, gaining 50% toward that $65k target. My hesitation cost me real opportunity: I had the directional thesis right but lacked the confidence to commit, revealing a gap between my analysis and my risk tolerance that I need to examine. The skip wasn't caution; it was indecision masquerading as prudence.

pending_suggestions.text: 2+ politics low_materiality misses — consider lowering materiality threshold by 0.05
